title: Arbitrary manual text live extraction fall-through
problem: Tickets 123–125 were docs-only; NM-4 fall-through plumbing landed in ticket-112 but live operator proof on arbitrary real text still yielded 0 accepted claims (2 rejected overgeneralized_scope). Product-risk reduction requires arbitrary real text → live Ollama → Python validator → at least one accepted graph row in a gitignored DB, without weakening mock/golden determinism.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh arbitrary .txt or .md source not in the fixture map can be ingested.
In explicit live mode, absent fixture checksum falls through to Ollama extraction instead of silently returning no candidates.
At least one validated accepted claim is persisted to a gitignored DB.
Rejections are preserved if produced; if none are produced, the report says so honestly.
Existing fixture/manual synthnote tests remain deterministic.
Golden tests remain mock-only and pass.
non_goals: Replacing fixture behavior in golden tests.
rollback_plan: Revert live calibration or prompt changes; retain ticket-112 fall-through gates.
notes: Hardened scope: ticket-112 landed --live-manual-fallthrough and mock fail-closed. ticket-127 completes NM-4 by achieving live accepted-claim proof on arbitrary real text (prompt/source calibration if needed), not re-plumbing the fall-through path.
